figures.caption: Table 2: Per-run evaluation costs on HAL benchmarks. Costs reflect API fees and vary with model and scaffold.

Figure 6: Task Selection Strategy × Benchmark (Mean ρ Across Protocols). Each cell shows the mean Spearman ρ across evaluation protocols; worst-case ρ is shown below in gray. Asterisks mark the best-performing strategy per benchmark.

Figure 7: Why Easiest-k ≈ MR: Task Difficulty Distributions. Each panel shows the task pass-rate density for one benchmark, with Easiest-k (green) and MR (orange) task sets overlaid. Dashed lines mark the 30–70% mid-range band. Panels are ordered by decreasing task overlap between the two strategies.
Table 4: Per-benchmark mean ρ across all evaluation protocols. Random reports mean ± std over 100 seeds per protocol; Worst/Best denote extreme seed outcomes across all protocols.

Figure 3: Performance Stability Across Distribution Shifts. Average Spearman ρ by task selection strategy, disaggregated across four evaluation protocols (LOAO, LOSO, Random 20%, and Temporal shift). Mid-Range selection maintains reliable rankings (> 0.85 mean ρ) regardless of the evaluation regime.
Table 3: Rank prediction (ρ, τ) vs. score prediction (R2) across evaluation protocols. ”Best” selects the top-performing task selection method per benchmark; ”Avg” averages across methods.

Table 5: Benchmark reduction from mid-range filtering. Task reduction is the fraction eliminated; cost savings (per agent, per benchmark) assume linear scaling with task count, using median agent cost from HAL leaderboards. TerminalBench lacks HAL-style cost data. USACO LOSO is unavailable because all but one agent use a single scaffold. SciCode excluded due to insufficient mid-range tasks (k = 4).

Figure 2: Range of Performance by Selection Strategy. Mean, best-case, and worst-case Spearman ρ across overlapping benchmarks and evaluation protocols. Mid-Range task selection offers the overall best ranking preservation across benchmarks.

Figure 5: Post-hoc sensitivity analysis of mid-range bands. The average Spearman rank correlation ρ (blue, left axis) and average task reduction percentage (red, right axis) across seven benchmarks for increasingly narrow mid-range bands. Shaded regions denote ±1 standard deviation across the benchmarks. The 30–70 band, chosen a priori, strikes a highly favorable balance.
Table 1: Benchmark statistics. Avg task ρ: mean pairwise Spearman correlation between task outcome vectors across agents; higher values indicate a more homogeneous benchmark.
